p1.speaker: narrator
p1.line: The manor parlor. Tea already poured. Two cups. She was expecting him — and Gary has stood at the study window; he knows what the wind does to a garden's sentences. This is the one primed scene that arrives HEAVIER, not lighter.

b1.speaker: narrator
b1.line: The manor parlor. Tea already poured. Two cups. She was expecting him. In this room, there is no off-the-record toggle to decline — the whole conversation simply is, and always will be, hers and his.

b2.speaker: beatrice
b2.line: Sit, Mr. Gibbons. You've been walking a spiral all week, and spirals end at their center. Ask.

b3.speaker: gary
b3.line: Did you take the contents of the capsule?

b6.speaker: narrator
b6.line: A pause exactly one breath long.

b7.speaker: beatrice
b7.line: I have a fear. A fear is not knowledge. I won't dress it up as one for your notebook.

b8.speaker: gary
b8.line: Then tell me about the will.

b9.speaker: narrator
b9.line: She turns her cup a quarter turn.

b10.speaker: beatrice
b10.line: I read it. Ten years ago, the night before Edmund sealed it away. That is all the further I will go. It is not mine to tell. It was his last act, and I will not spend it for him.

b11.speaker: gary
b11.line: Mrs. Vale. Something started this. Something small. In my experience it's always something small.

b12.speaker: narrator
b12.line: A long look. She decides he's earned a true thing.

b13.speaker: beatrice
b13.line: ...Five nights before the ceremony, in the garden, my daughter said something to me that broke my heart a little. She said, "It isn't fair. Everyone already knows he'll lead." And because I could not tell her what I knew, I gave her the only true thing I had that wasn't a secret. I said: "You may be surprised."

b14.speaker: beatrice
b14.line: You've just understood something. I can see it land. Be careful with it, Mr. Gibbons. Whatever it is — it's carrying one of my children.

b15.speaker: gary
b15.line: Mrs. Vale. My grandfather had a rule about stories like this one. "Truth with compassion. In that order — and no other."

b16.speaker: beatrice
b16.line: Then Gary Gibbons raised you correctly. Good evening.
